---
description: Before commiting and pushing your code, please ensure that you have followed the project's coding guidelines and best practices. This includes writing clean, maintainable code, adhering to the project's style guide, and including appropriate comments and documentation where necessary. Additionally, make sure to run all tests and ensure they pass before submitting your changes.
Particularly check that all dependencies are properly declared and that your code does not introduce any security vulnerabilities. If you are unsure about any aspect of the coding guidelines, please refer to the project's documentation or reach out to the maintainers for clarification.
Also check that all method used are declared and that there are no unused variables or imports in your code. This helps to keep the codebase clean and efficient.
Then after you have reviewed your code and are confident that it meets the project's standards, you can proceed to commit and push your changes to the repository. Remember to write clear and concise commit messages that accurately describe the changes you have made, as this will help other contributors understand the purpose of your changes and facilitate collaboration within the project.
after commit and push, wait for github workflow to be finished and check the results. If there are any issues or errors, address them promptly to ensure the stability and quality of the codebase. Then download the latest version of the files and zip them into downloads\Photobooth-windows-latestt.zip and upload it to the releases section of the repository, making sure to include a clear description of the changes and improvements included in this release. This will help users understand the benefits of updating to the latest version and encourage them to do so.
# applyTo: 'Describe when these instructions should be loaded' # when provided, instructions will automatically be added to the request context when the pattern matches an attached file
---
See readme.md for instructions on how to contribute to the project and ensure that your code meets the project's standards before committing and pushing your changes.
